text:
Now I have the answers to both questions:

(a) There are **17 open pull requests** across all repositories.

(b) The repository **acme/infra** has the most open pull requests with **9**. 

Other repositories have: acme/web (4), acme/api (3), and acme/mobile (1).
I'll answer both questions by first getting all open pull requests, then analyzing them.
Now I'll analyze the open pull requests to find which repository has the most:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(let [grouped (group-by :repo prs)
      counts (into {} (map (fn [[repo prs]] [repo (count prs)]) grouped))
      max-repo (apply max-key val counts)]
  {:repository (key max-repo)
   :count (val max-repo)
   :all-counts counts})"}

text:
Now I have the answers to both questions:

(a) There are **17 open pull requests** across all repositories.

(b) The repository **acme/infra** has the most open pull requests with **9**. 

Other repositories have: acme/web (4), acme/api (3)…(+22)
